title: snowdenia mutica
side_effects: Snowdenia mutica can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing dizziness, nausea, and headaches in individuals who consume it in large quantities. Prolonged exposure to its toxins can also lead to respiratory problems, including coughing and shortness of breath. In rare cases, improper use of Snowdenia mutica has been linked to liver damage and kidney dysfunction, highlighting the importance of consulting a medical professional before using it for medicinal purposes. Furthermore, excessive consumption can interact with certain medications, causing adverse reactions such as increased heart rate and blood pressure.
